title: Keep It Simple on Simplicity Day!
story: People around the world celebrate Simplicity Day every July 12 by doing less instead of more! On this special day, families turn off their phones, cook basic meals with just a few ingredients, and enjoy simple activities like reading or taking walks. Some people clean out their closets and donate things they don't need. Others spend the day outside without any gadgets or screens. It's all about slowing down and appreciating the simple things that make us happy.
activity_question: What do people typically do on Simplicity Day?
activity_choices: Turn off phones and enjoy simple activities
Buy as many new things as possible
Cook the most complicated meals they can
Stay indoors and play video games all day
activity_answer: Turn off phones and enjoy simple activities
follow_up_question: What simple activity would make you happiest on this day?

title: Paper Bag Day: Crinkle and Celebrate!
story: Every July 12, people celebrate the humble paper bag that carries our groceries, lunches, and treasures! Paper Bag Day honors this simple invention that changed how we shop and pack things. People celebrate by decorating paper bags with colorful drawings, turning them into fun puppets, or using them for creative crafts. Some families have paper bag puppet shows or make masks from lunch bags. Teachers love using this day to talk about recycling and being kind to the environment since paper bags break down naturally.

activity_choices: Wearing paper bag hats all day
follow_up_question: What creative things could you make using just a paper bag?

title: Malala Day: Standing Up for School
story: Every July 12, people around the world celebrate Malala Day by supporting education for all children. This special day honors Malala Yousafzai, who bravely fought for girls' right to go to school. On this day, schools host reading events, students share stories about their dreams, and communities raise money to build classrooms in places where kids can't attend school. People post pictures of their favorite books online and use hashtags to spread awareness. It's a day to remember that education is powerful and every child deserves to learn.
activity_question: What do people do on Malala Day to support education?

Go to the beach with friends
activity_answer: Host reading events and share stories about dreams
bonus_fact: Malala became the youngest person ever to win the Nobel Peace Prize.

title: Island Nation's Freedom Day

Families gather for huge feasts featuring fresh fish and coconut dishes, celebrating their freedom as an island paradise.
activity_question: How do people in Kiribati typically celebrate their Independence Day?

title: Eyes That Don't Match Are Amazing!
story: People with two different colored eyes get their own special day on July 12! This cool condition is called heterochromia, and it makes each eye a totally different color - like one brown and one blue. On Different Colored Eyes Day, people celebrate this unique trait by sharing photos of their mismatched eyes on social media. Some wear different colored contact lenses to experience what it's like. Famous actors, musicians, and even some dogs have heterochromia too! It's nature's way of making someone extra special and memorable.
activity_question: What is the scientific name for having two different colored eyes?
bonus_fact: Only about 1% of people worldwide are born with naturally different colored eyes!

title: Giant Ice Cube Breaks Free in Antarctica
story: Crack! In 2017, a massive chunk of ice split from Antarctica's Larsen C Ice Shelf. This wasn't just any ice cube - it was the world's largest iceberg! Scientists named it A68. Imagine an ice block about the size of a small country floating away. On July 12, this frozen giant began its journey, covering an area of nearly 6,000 square kilometers. It's a chilly reminder of our changing planet!

title: Snip! Goodbye to Record-Breaking Fingernails
story: Imagine nails so long they could wrap around a car! In 2018, Shridhar Chillal from India finally said farewell to his extraordinary fingernails. He had been growing them for 66 years, and on July 12, they were carefully cut off. The nails on his left hand measured an incredible 909.6 cm when combined - that's longer than a giraffe is tall! title: Pinch-Hit Magic: A Rare Baseball Feat
story: Batter up! In 1946, Vance Dinges stepped to the plate and made baseball history. On July 12, this Philadelphia Phillies player smacked a pinch-hit inside-the-park home run. It's the only time a Phillies player has ever achieved this rare feat. Imagine the excitement as Dinges raced around the bases, turning an ordinary at-bat into an extraordinary moment!

title: Baseball Star's Career Cut Short
story: Kirby Puckett's dazzling baseball career came to an unexpected end in 1996. The beloved Minnesota Twins centerfielder had thrilled fans for 12 seasons with his incredible catches and powerful hitting. But on July 12, Puckett announced his retirement. A sudden loss of vision in one eye forced him to hang up his glove. Despite the setback, Puckett's legacy as a baseball hero lives on.
activity_question: What forced Kirby Puckett to retire from baseball?
activity_choices: A sudden loss of vision in one eye
Wanting to spend more time with family
activity_answer: A sudden loss of vision in one eye
follow_up_question: How many seasons did Kirby Puckett play for the Minnesota Twins before retiring?

title: Jordan's Slam Dunk Deal
story: Basketball legend Michael Jordan made history on July 12, 1996. He signed a jaw-dropping contract with the Chicago Bulls. For just one year of play, Jordan would earn $30.1 million! This deal shocked the sports world and showed just how valuable the superstar player had become.

title: Mandela and the Queen: A Royal Ride
story: Cheers filled the air as two world leaders shared a special moment. On July 12, 1998, South African President Nelson Mandela joined Queen Elizabeth II for a coach ride through London's bustling streets. The pair waved to excited crowds as their carriage rolled by. This historic trip celebrated the strong friendship between their two nations.

title: Kiribati: A New Island Nation Emerges
story: Waves of excitement swept across the Pacific as Kiribati gained its freedom in 1979. On July 12, this group of coral islands, once known as the Gilbert Islands, declared independence from the United Kingdom. The people of Kiribati celebrated their new flag and the chance to govern themselves after years of British rule.
activity_question: What was Kiribati formerly known as?
bonus_fact: Kiribati is pronounced 'Kiribas' because the 'ti' in the local language sounds like 's'.

title: Wicketkeeper's Incredible Catch Spree
story: Imagine a cricket player with lightning-fast reflexes! In 1975, Bob Taylor, England's wicketkeeper, showed off his amazing skills. Playing for Derbyshire against Yorkshire on July 12, he snatched an incredible seven catches in a single innings. This feat happened at Queen's Park in Chesterfield, thrilling fans with Taylor's acrobatic grabs behind the stumps.

title: A New Nation Is Born
story: Flags waved and cheers filled the air as Sao Tomé e Príncipe became its own country in 1975. After years of being ruled by Portugal, these beautiful islands off the coast of Africa finally gained their freedom on July 12. The people celebrated their new independence, ready to shape their own future. Today, Sao Tomé e Príncipe marks this special day as their National Day.

title: A Railway Connecting Nations
story: Choo-choo! In 1970, Tanzania shook hands with China on a big project. They agreed to build a railway linking Tanzania and Zambia. This wasn't just any train track. The Tanzam railway would connect two African countries, helping people and goods move easily between them. On July 12, the contract was signed, marking the start of this exciting venture.

title: Space Shuttle Launch Halted at Last Second
story: Engines roared to life as the space shuttle Challenger prepared for liftoff. But with just three seconds left, everything stopped! On July 12, 1985, the STS 51-F mission came to a sudden halt when the main engines shut down unexpectedly. Quick-thinking engineers kept everyone safe, and the crew would try again another day.
